markdown.markdown: ::: {.compare-page} ::: {.compare-hero} training and architecture axis This page compares the models where the **training objective, sampling strategy, physical constraint or architecture** changed. It intentionally excludes pure inference sweeps such as solver, step count, sigma and tile overlap. ::: ## EKE Spectral View The final version will use one LIR-generated plot with all EKE spectra on the same axes. For now, the figure below is a placeholder so we can validate the layout before synchronizing real diagnostics. ![Placeholder EKE intercomparison across OceanLens training variants.](assets/figures/compare/eke_intercomparison_placeholder.svg){.wide-figure} ## Benchmark Matrix ::: {.metric-disclaimer} The values below are placeholders. They are here to design the table and will be replaced by metrics computed from LIR outputs on the same 2004 test day and domain. ::: ::: {.scoreboard} | Model | Change | RMSE ↓ | Bias ↓ | Std ratio ↑ | EKE ↑ | Status | |---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---| | v8a DiT | pixel transformer | 0.71 | 0.03 | 0.82 | 0.86 | promising, artifact check | | v7a Swin | local window attention | 0.74 | 0.04 | 0.78 | 0.83 | strong candidate | | Recoarsen FT | LR consistency | 0.79 | 0.02 | 0.70 | 0.76 | physical guardrail | | Regional FT | weighted patches | 0.81 | 0.05 | 0.72 | 0.74 | regional repair | | CFG FT | condition dropout | 0.84 | 0.06 | 0.76 | 0.72 | needs scale tuning | | v4 S1 | OT + logit-normal t | 0.88 | 0.04 | 0.62 | 0.66 | baseline | | CNO only | deterministic mean | 0.93 | 0.03 | 0.42 | 0.51 | too smooth | | LR input | coarse reference | 1.18 | 0.08 | 0.18 | 0.22 | reference | ::: ## What This Table Should Answer ::: {.comparison-grid} ::: {.comparison-card} ### Accuracy Does the model reduce RMSE against HR without simply smoothing the residual field? ::: ::: {.comparison-card} ### Energy Does the generated field recover the HR EKE spectrum, especially at the mesoscale and submesoscale frequencies? ::: ::: {.comparison-card} ### Physical consistency Does the generated HR field remain compatible with the LR field after re-coarsening? ::: ::: {.comparison-card} ### Regional robustness Does the model behave better in difficult regions such as the Mediterranean, Black Sea, Arctic and energetic western boundary currents? ::: ::: ## Replacement Plan 1. Pull the finished `.npz`, metrics CSV and TensorBoard curves from LIR. 2. Generate one EKE spectrum figure for `thetao`, with HR, LR, CNO and all architecture variants. 3. Replace placeholder metrics with measured RMSE, bias, residual std ratio, EKE score and re-coarsening error. 4. Keep inference-only sweeps in version pages, not in this architecture table. :::
